error.signature: FileNotFoundError: No such file or directory — subprocess fails on path with spaces

verdict.summary: subprocess.run() with shell=False splits path containing spaces into multiple arguments instead of treating it as one path.

dead_ends.why_fails: os.system() is less secure (shell injection) and has the same quoting issues

dead_ends.action: Add backslash escapes to the path string
dead_ends.why_fails: Backslash escaping is shell-level; subprocess with shell=False does not interpret shell escapes

workarounds.action: Pass command as a list of separate arguments, not a single string
workarounds.how: subprocess.run(['ls', '/path/with spaces/file.txt'])  # list form, not string

workarounds.action: If shell=True is needed, use shlex.quote() to properly escape paths
